The individual known as '''Wikyochee''' belonged to a vaguely humanoid species. Ten years of age, he was in possession of an [[angry machine]], which allowed him to calm his turbulent demeanor, and resort to a more placid temper.

==Biography==
Wikyochee was an individual member of a vaguely humanoid species. At ten years of age, he possessed what was termed an "[[angry machine]]," that would be activated and put to use whenever he was in a particularly tumultuous mood. The angry machine would calm and soothe Wikyochee's body, allowing him to abandon his more incensed temperament in favor of a more affable demeanor. His right leg was made of an alloy, which consisted of metal and [[Carbonite/Legends|carbonite]]. Wikyochee also had small items cuffed to the two limbs that stemmed from his scaly thighs.<ref name="daa" />

==Personality and traits==
Wikyochee possessed a particularly pleasant demeanor, although he was sometimes prone to fits of rage. He could, however, be placated by utilizing his angry machine. He possessed [[Color/Legends|brown]] fur, and green scales.<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
Wikyochee was created by [[Aaron Reeder]], an 11-year-old ''[[Star Wars]]'' fan, through the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition held by [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine|''Star Wars Galaxy'']] magazine, which was launched in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 3]] ([[1995]]). The competition called for readers to create entirely original aliens influenced and inspired by the [[original trilogy]] of films from the ''Star Wars'' saga. Reeder developed his creation by drawing on three characters from the original trilogy—[[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]] the [[Ewok/Legends|Ewok]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]], and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo's]] sidekick [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. By amalgamating the characters, Reeder came up with Wikyochee. His entry was not a winner in the competition, but it was nonetheless published in [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|''Star Wars Galaxy Magazine'' 7]] alongside the winners under the "honorable mentions" category of the "under 16" subdivision.<ref name="daa" /> ''Star Wars'' continuity editor [[Leland Chee]] later stated that the successfully published "Design an Alien" material was to be considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref>
